reconstructed_sketch: From chapter context (the entire sthita-prajña block 2.54-2.71 building toward a terminal state), a verse at position 2.72 — the chapter's last verse — would clearly be expected to name the terminus. A competent writer would reach for 'this is the state of the realised one' or 'attaining this, one is liberated'. They would NOT, without seeing the verse, produce the specific compound brāhmī sthitiḥ as the terminus name, nor brahma-nirvāṇa as the terminal liberation, nor the anta-kāle 'pi anti-deathbed-anxiety force, nor the eṣā chapter-closing back-reference. The chapter context provides the slot but not the technical-term filling.
verse_specific_signal: The chapter-closing back-reference function (eṣā as 'this' referring back to the entire chapter) is unique to the chapter's closing verse. The brāhmī sthiti and brahma-nirvāṇa technical terms are unique to 2.72 in this chapter (and brāhmī-sthiti is a first-occurrence in the Gita). The anta-kāle 'pi structural force is unique. The engineering analog's STRETCHED tag and operational scope-down are licensed by the verse's specific metaphysical maximum-loading.
verdict_reason: Score 1 — chapter context provides only the slot; the verse's specific technical terms and structural functions are not reconstructible from the chapter alone. The engineering layer's correct treatment (operational scoping with explicit STRETCHED tag) requires the verse itself. Pass.
